pattern: Reachability among the jump targets is a graph search from the start index, where visiting a zero-valued cell answers the question and a visited set prevents looping.
hints: From any index you have at most two outgoing moves: forward by its value and backward by its value, as long as you stay in bounds.
This is a reachability question on an implicit graph, so a breadth-first or depth-first traversal from the start works.
Track visited indices to avoid cycles; if you ever stand on a cell whose value is zero, the answer is yes, otherwise no once the search is exhausted.

edge_inputs.description: reachable zero from a back-of-array start
every jump lands on a value that bounces away from the zero
must use a backward jump to reach the zero

stress.description: large array with a planted reachable zero from the start
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50000))
    arr = [rng.randint(0, n - 1) for _ in range(n)]
    start = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    return [arr, start]
stress.note: random values may or may not reach a zero; oracle agreement validates either outcome
